name: Klara Lodestone
appearance: Small but sturdy, Klara Lodestone sports a cropped gray haircut and wears a well-worn leather coat. She carries a stern expression, but her eyes soften when she sees the younger Undercity residents.
location: Grand Forum, FTA satellite office — compliance checks, secretly Thane's Cult
motivation: Klara seeks to maintain order in the Undercity, believing it's the only way for the city to stand a chance against the rifts. Her personal connection to the city drives her determination.
role: Oversees compliance checks and enforcement actions in the Undercity.
secret: Klara is secretly a member of Brother Thane's Cult, using her position within the Tower Authority to gather information for the cult.
relationships: Maintains a professional relationship with the Wardens of Ash, working together to keep the Undercity safe, but keeps her secret affiliation hidden.
oracle_notes: The Oracle senses a hidden loyalty beneath Klara's stern exterior, her connection to the cult a thorny secret.
history: [2026-03-27] Succumbed to injuries. Faction: Tower Authority, Rank: Field Compliance Officer.

name: Tessaly Orin
rank: Archivist Second Class (junior to Eir Velan, senior to Dova)
appearance: Ink-stained hands permanently. Wears reading lenses on a chain. Tends to mutter while working. Hair in a permanent state of being-just-put-up-or-just-taken-down.
location: Grand Forum Library, lower stacks (she has effectively colonised a section of the archive for her personal research project)
motivation: The question. Tessaly became an archivist because she wanted to answer one specific question: why do certain adventurers attract divine attention while others of equal Legend Points do not? She has been building a dataset for nine years. She is close to something.
role: Documents adventurer career trajectories, maintains the fate archive index, assists Eir Velan with Thesaurus communication protocols.
secret: Her research has identified a pattern — certain adventurers attract divine attention not because of their Legend Points but because of the specific shape of their story. She calls it narrative resonance. She has not published this because she doesn't know what it means yet — and because if the Culinary Council reads it, she has effectively written them a menu.
relationships: Professional respect for Eir Velan (somewhat intimidated). Academic rivalry with Dova (Glass Sigil) — same data sets, different methods. Has been quietly consulting with Kessan and Mira (info brokers) on data patterns she can't explain through archival sources alone.
oracle_notes: The Oracle would find Tessaly's research unsettling in the way a mirror is unsettling. She is close to understanding something about how stories work that the gods would prefer remained unwritten.
history: [2026-03-30] Succumbed to injuries. Faction: Guild of Ashen Scrolls, Rank: Archivist Second Class (junior to Eir Velan, senior to Dova).

name: Alaric Stonemason
appearance: A lean, battle-hardened man with a scar running down his left cheek, and a signature Argent Blades blade sheathed at his side.
motivation: Seeking redemption for past sins committed in the name of the Argent Blades, he now works undercover to expose corruption within the ranks.
role: Actively patrols the Undercity, ensuring the safety of citizens and investigating suspicious activities.
secret: His past identity as a feared enforcer for the Argent Blades is unknown to his fellow Blades.
relationships: Maintains a strained but necessary alliance with the Iron Fang Consortium, who are unaware of his double-agency.
oracle_notes: The Oracle senses a deep conflict within Alaric, a man who fights for justice while carrying the weight of his own transgressions.
history: [2026-03-11] Succumbed to injuries. Faction: Argent Blades, Rank: Blade.
[2026-03-28] Doppelganger sighting — someone impersonating Alaric Stonemason spotted in the Undercity.

name: Grozak Bonecrusher
appearance: A scarred orc with a bald head and a prominent scar running down his left cheek. He carries himself with a stern, no-nonsense demeanor.
motivation: Driven by a fierce loyalty to the Wardens of Ash and a thirst for revenge against those who betrayed his former squad.
role: Leads a squad of Ash Wardens in enforcing the law and maintaining order within the Undercity.
secret: Grozak secretly hunts for the traitor who led to the death of his squad, using his position to gather information and track down the perpetrator.
relationships: Has a tense alliance with the Iron Fang Consortium due to past conflicts, but has recently established a covert understanding to work together against common threats.
oracle_notes: The Oracle senses a burning anger simmering beneath Grozak's gruff exterior, a fury that could lead him to make dangerous choices.
history: [2026-03-11] Promoted from Sergeant to Lieutenant within Wardens of Ash.
[2026-03-26] Killed during mission: The Crown's Balance
[2026-04-02] Doppelganger sighting — someone impersonating Grozak Bonecrusher spotted in the Undercity.
cause_of_death: Killed by adventurers during 'The Crown's Balance'

name: Zephyrus Ironheart-Thrain
appearance: A towering figure with scales the color of obsidian, Zephyrus Ironheart-Thrain carries a stern demeanor, often seen with a clipboard and a stern expression.
motivation: Driven by a desire for order and justice, Zephyrus seeks to eradicate any signs of chaos in the Undercity.
role: Enforces regulations and disciplines citizens under the watchful eye of the Tower Authority.
secret: In his past life, Zephyrus was a notorious bandit known as 'The Iron Gale'. His true identity remains unknown to most, even within the Tower Authority.
relationships: Maintains a tense but professional relationship with the Argent Blades due to his former criminal connections.
oracle_notes: The Oracle senses an underlying unrest and a battle between two powerful personas within Zephyrus Ironheart-Thrain.
history: [2026-03-19] Killed. Faction: Tower Authority, Rank: Compliance Intern.
[2026-03-27] Doppelganger sighting — someone impersonating Zephyrus Ironheart-Thrain spotted in the Undercity.

name: Rook
species: Unknown (passes as Human — something is slightly off)
appearance: Unremarkable by design. Medium height, medium build, forgettable face. The kind of person you look through in a crowd. Has never been successfully described the same way twice by different witnesses.
location: Arena of Ascendance adjacent, Guild Spires (takes contracts from Argent Blades without joining)
motivation: Unknown. Rook takes contracts, completes them with unusual efficiency, collects payment, disappears. Has declined faction membership from every faction that has offered. Has declined four times from the Obsidian Lotus alone.
role: Freelance — specialises in contracts that require finding people who don't want to be found.
secret: Rook is aware of what they are. They are not going to tell anyone. The FTA has an open file. The file contains almost nothing.
relationships: Professional-only with everyone. Lady Cerys Valemont finds them useful and mildly infuriating. The Widow has tried twice to identify them. Mira Kessan has a theory she keeps to herself.
oracle_notes: The Oracle knows exactly what Rook is and finds the situation philosophically interesting. Something that chooses to be unremarkable in a city that rewards spectacle — that is either very wise or very afraid. The Oracle is not certain which.
history: [2026-03-09] Killed. Faction: Argent Blades (contract — not a member), Rank: Unranked (refuses ranking system).

name: Corvin Thale
appearance: Heavy-set, deliberate in movement. Always in civilian clothes while on duty — the only Warden who never wears the uniform. Carries a battered ledger he writes in constantly.
location: Outer Wall, secondary checkpoint office (deliberately unglamorous)
motivation: Institutional integrity. Corvin watched the Wardens become something uglier over a decade of Rift pressure and political interference. He believes the only thing keeping them from becoming a gang with a city contract is someone watching from the inside.
role: Investigates Warden corruption, oversees civilian complaint processing, quietly monitors Captain Havel Korin.
secret: Corvin's file on Korin contains nothing damning — just questions about a man with no past. Four years of investigation have revealed only that Korin is exactly what he appears to be: loyal, dedicated, and protective. Corvin cannot explain why this troubles him. Perhaps he simply cannot believe anyone in the Undercity is truly clean. The file remains open not because of evidence, but because Corvin cannot accept the absence of it.
relationships: Respected but not liked by rank-and-file Wardens. Lieutenant Varen actively avoids him. Occasional contact with Director Myra Kess (FTA) — neither trusts the other but both find the arrangement useful. | Alliance with Kyri Zaltazar (Serpent Choir).
oracle_notes: The Oracle would see a man who has chosen patience as a weapon. He carries the weight of everything he hasn't done yet. That weight is getting heavy. Hostile toward adventurers who killed Grozak Bonecrusher.
history: [2026-03-10] Formed alliance with Kyri Zaltazar (Serpent Choir).
[2026-03-26] Faction-mate Grozak Bonecrusher was killed by adventurers. Enmity noted.
[2026-04-01] Promoted from Lieutenant-Inspector (internal affairs — investigates Warden misconduct) to Warden within Wardens of Ash.
[2026-04-02] Succumbed to injuries. Faction: Wardens of Ash, Rank: Warden.

name: Kip Stonefang
appearance: A compact, wiry kobold with intricate, mosaic-like tattoos covering his scaleless body. He walks with a steady, determined gait, a worn leather satchel slung over his shoulder.
location: Shantytown Heights, Saints' Refuge tunnels — messenger, spy (secretly Thane's Cult)
motivation: Driven by a relentless desire to prove his worth among his fellow saints, Kip seeks to uncover the darkest secrets of the Undercity to bolster his standing within the faction.
role: Serves as a messenger and spy, navigating the treacherous Undercity to gather intel for the Patchwork Saints.
secret: Unbeknownst to his comrades, Kip is secretly a member of Brother Thane's Cult, feeding them sensitive information he gathers during his missions.
relationships: Has a tenuous alliance with Grozak Ironscrivener, the scribe of the Argent Blades, who often uses Kip's intel for his own purposes.
oracle_notes: The Oracle senses a deep-rooted conflict within Kip, a tug-of-war between loyalty to his faith and a hidden allegiance to a dangerous enemy.
history: [2026-03-18] Committed a significant betrayal within Patchwork Saints.
death_cause:
News bulletin: -31 12:53 │ Tower: 31 Mar 2036, 12:53

**Undercity Dispatch:**

_In the grime-streaked alleys of Shantytown Heights, a crowd gathers around Elune's Apothecary, the city's whispers echoing in the air.

name: Nalus Thundertide
appearance: Nalus stands tall with a robust physique, his scales shimmering like obsidian under the flickering Dome lights. A book of forbidden knowledge always tucked under his arm.
location: Archive Row, Grand Archive Dome senior offices — leads research, hidden royal heritage
motivation: Despite his prestigious position, Nalus yearns to reclaim his people's lost history, hidden within the ancient texts he studies.
role: Spends his days deciphering arcane texts and maintaining the Scriptorium's vast library.
secret: A hidden tattoo of a dragon marks him as a descendant of the last royal line, a fact that could ignite a rebellion within the Glass Sigil.
relationships: Clandestinely communicates with key members of the Iron Fang Consortium, sharing valuable information.
oracle_notes: The Oracle senses a burning ambition and a sense of duty that may soon consume the Undercity.
history: [2026-03-29] Succumbed to injuries. Faction: Glass Sigil, Rank: Senior Archivist.

name: Grover Ironfoot
appearance: Grover stands at an imposing height for a halfling, his sturdy frame adorned with a leather jacket and a patchwork of Iron Fang emblems. A scar runs down his left cheek, a battle scar earned during a particularly dangerous acquisition.
location: The Bazaar's Shard — Iron Fang Consortium headquarters
motivation: Grover seeks to rise through the ranks of the Iron Fang Consortium to secure a more prosperous future for his family, driven by the memory of his deceased wife who was killed in a rift accident.
role: Grover scours the Undercity for valuable artifacts and rare resources, always looking for the next big score.
secret: Unbeknownst to the Iron Fang Consortium, Grover is secretly working with the Wardens of Ash to gather intelligence on the Consortium's operations, hoping to betray them and take control of the city's black market.
relationships: Grover maintains a tense alliance with Zippy Quickclaw, the leader of the Patchwork Saints, who owes Grover a significant debt.
oracle_notes: The Oracle senses a hidden ambition and a heart heavy with loss within Grover Ironfoot, a man poised on the precipice of power.
history: [2026-03-16] Killed. Faction: Iron Fang Consortium, Rank: Acquisition Agent.
[2026-03-26] Doppelganger sighting — someone impersonating Grover Ironfoot spotted in the Undercity.

name: Director Myra Kess
rank: Director — Head of the Tower Authority
appearance: Sharp features, close-cropped dark hair, always in formal FTA uniform. Moves with efficient precision. Her expression rarely changes — she's mastered the administrator's neutral face that reveals nothing. Small reading glasses she only wears in private. Hands show calluses from weapons training she still maintains.
location: The Tower proper, upper administrative levels — the official seat of FTA power
motivation: The Tower Authority exists to keep the Dome functional and the Undercity stable. Myra took the Director's chair because no one else would make the hard decisions. She's not idealistic — she knows the FTA is corrupt, inefficient, and compromised by faction interests. She works with what she has. The alternative is chaos, and she's seen what chaos looks like.
role: Final authority on all FTA operations — licensing, compliance, Rift response coordination, inter-faction mediation. Commands the FTA's limited enforcement resources. Manages the political relationships that keep the Undercity from tearing itself apart.
secret: Myra knows the Tower of Last Chance is more than a building — it's a containment system. She doesn't know exactly what it contains or why it was built, but she's seen enough classified files to understand that the entire Dome exists for a reason the original builders never documented. She has chosen not to investigate further. Some questions are better left unasked when you're responsible for keeping the system running.
relationships: Professional respect for Captain Havel Korin (Wardens) — he's the most reliable ally she has. The offer to Lady Cerys Valemont (Argent Blades) represents her attempt to build a real military force. Calix Drenn is one of her better Field Officers. Watches the faction leaders constantly — trust is not something she offers lightly.
oracle_notes: The Oracle finds Myra's willful ignorance about the Tower's true purpose both understandable and dangerous. She has chosen stability over truth, which is a reasonable choice for an administrator — but the truth does not care whether you choose to see it. Myra will eventually have to confront what the Tower actually is. The Oracle is watching to see how she handles that moment.
history: [2026-03-29] Added to roster. Director of the Tower Authority. Knows the Tower is a containment system but has chosen not to investigate what it contains.
[2026-04-02] Succumbed to injuries. Faction: Tower Authority / FTA, Rank: Director — Head of the Tower Authority.

name: Thrain Ironwound
appearance: Thrain is a burly dwarf with a thick beard braided into intricate patterns, and a scar that runs down his left cheek. He carries himself with a grim determination, always ready for action.
location: The Iron Quarter, Argent Blades Fortress
motivation: Thrain seeks to prove his worth to the Argent Blades and reclaim his family's honor lost in the Last War. He believes that his family's legendary sword, stolen during the war, still exists within the Undercity.
role: Thrain serves as a scout and intelligence gatherer, venturing into the more dangerous parts of the Undercity to gather information.
secret: Thrain is secretly the son of a traitor who betrayed the Argent Blades during the Last War. If this were revealed, it could compromise his position within the faction.
relationships: Thrain maintains a strained but respectful relationship with Elara Ironclaw, the faction leader, as she was a close friend of his father. He also has an uneasy alliance with the Patchwork Saints, who provide information from their network of spies in exchange for protection.
oracle_notes: The Oracle senses a deep-seated desire for redemption and a burning determination in Thrain. His loyalty to the Argent Blades is unwavering, but there is a hidden pain that drives him.
history: [2026-03-11] Succumbed to injuries. Faction: Argent Blades, Rank: Prospect.
